zh.Ardee, County Louth, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 盧斯阿爾迪
zh.Of, Elford, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾艾爾福德
zh.Lathom St. James, Lathom, Lancashire, En: 英國藍卡爾拉堂姆 聖 詹姆斯
zh.Burscough Priory, Burscough, West Lancas: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾布爾斯庫
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD於1362年6月2日出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾時，他的父親William de Stanley33歲，母親Alice De Massey24歲。他於大約1385年與Isabel de Latham在英國英格蘭藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1414年1月6日在愛爾蘭庫迪 盧斯阿爾迪享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾布爾斯庫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD于1362年6月2日出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾时，他的父亲William de Stanley33岁，母亲Alice De Massey24岁。他于大约1385年与Isabel de Latham在英国英格兰藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1414年1月6日在爱尔兰庫迪 盧斯阿爾迪享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾布爾斯庫。

nameMeanings.name: Stanley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Stanley, including those in Yorkshire, Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Durham, Gloucestershire, and Wiltshire, named with Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. This English name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century where it was Gaelicized as de Stainléigh.

English: possibly also a variant of Stoneley, a habitational name from Stoneley Green in Burland (Cheshire), Stoneleigh (Warwickshire), or Stonely in Kimbolton (Huntingdonshire), all named with Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
Americanized form of any of various like-sounding names in other languages, for example Polish Stanislawski , Greek Anastasiou , and Serbian Stojadinov (patronymic from the personal name Stojadin).

variants.name: Stanislawski
meanings: Polish (Stanisławski): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Stanisław, Stanisławów, or Stanisławice, named with the personal name Stanisław (see Stanislav ).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the title of nobility, along the lines of Earl and Duke . The title arose in the Norman feudal system and seems to be of Germanic origin, derived from Old English beorn ‘young warrior’ or a related continental Germanic form. In part the given name, and in particular the variant Barron , may represent the surname derived from the title.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Lathom
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Latham
meanings: English: habitational name from Lathom in Lancashire, or occasionally perhaps from Laytham in Yorkshire. The placenames mean ‘at the barns’, from hlǫthum, the dative plural form of Old Norse hlatha ‘barn, store house’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD was born on 2 June 1362, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Stanley, was 33 and his mother, Alice De Massey, was 24. He married Isabel de Latham about 1385, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 6 January 1414, in Ardee, County Louth, Ireland, at the age of 51, and was buried in Burscough, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD est né le 2 juin 1362, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Stanley, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Alice De Massey, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Isabel de Latham environ 1385, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 6 janvier 1414, à Ardee, County Louth, Irlande, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Burscough, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD am 2. Juni 1362 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Stanley 33 und seine Mutter Alice De Massey 24. Er hat um 1385 in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Isabel de Latham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 6. Januar 1414 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Ardee, County Louth, Irland und wurde in Burscough, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD è nato il il 2 giugno 1362 nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Stanley, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Alice De Massey, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Isabel de Latham circa 1385, nel Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 6 gennaio 1414, nel Ardee, County Louth, Irlanda, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Burscough, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORDが1362年6月2日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Stanleyは33歳，母Alice De Masseyは24歳であった。約1385年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアでIsabel de Lathamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1414年1月6日にアイルランドコウントイ ロウスアルディーで51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアブルスコウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD(이)가 1362년 6월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Stanley(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice De Massey(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1385년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 Isabel de Latham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1414년 1월 6일 아일랜드 카운티 루스 아디에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 버스코우그h에 묻혔다.

pt.2 June 1362–6 January 1414: 2 de junho de 1362–6 de janeiro de 1414
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD nasceu em 2 de junho de 1362, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Stanley, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Alice De Massey, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel de Latham aproximadamente 1385, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1414, em Ardee, County Louth, Irlanda, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Burscough, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD родился 2 июня 1362 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Stanley было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Alice De Massey, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel de Latham около 1385 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 6 января 1414 г. в местности арди, коунти лоут, Ирландия в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности бурскоую, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.2 June 1362–6 January 1414: 2 de junio de 1362–6 de enero de 1414
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John II Knight Baron of Lathom Stanley LORD nació el 2 de junio de 1362, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Stanley, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Alice De Massey, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Isabel de Latham aproximadamente 1385, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 6 de enero de 1414, en Ardee, County Louth, Irlanda, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Burscough, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
